pi_items.source: setup_task.sh contains hardcoded Python script that generates ODS file with OpenDocumentSpreadsheet library
setup_task.sh lines 50-61 contain hardcoded array with exactly 10 rebate_data entries
setup_task.sh line 41 defines headers array with 8 elements: Product, Purchase Date, Rebate Amount, Validity Period, Deadline, Days Remaining, Status, Notes
Sum of Rebate Amount column from hardcoded data: 150+75+30+50+40+60+25+100+20+15
setup_task.sh data contains both MM/DD/YYYY (e.g., 11/15/2024) and DD-MM-YYYY (e.g., 12-01-2024) formats
Data analysis found MM/DD/YYYY format (9 rows) and DD-MM-YYYY format (1 row: 12-01-2024)
Data shows 4 entries with $ symbol and 6 without: $150, 75, $30.00, $50, 40, 60, $25, 100, 20, 15
setup_task.sh data rows 2,3,5,7,9 have empty deadline (column 5)
setup_task.sh data shows all 10 data rows have empty Days Remaining (column 6)
setup_task.sh line 88 saves as .ods format using OpenDocumentSpreadsheet library
task.json structure analysis shows keys: id, version, env_id, description, difficulty, natural_language, init, hooks, success. NO 'metadata' field exists.

privileged_info_summary: The rebate_deadline_tracker task has NO metadata field in task.json. The task data is entirely synthetic, generated by a Python script during setup using the ODF library to create an ODS file. The spreadsheet contains 10 hardcoded rebate entries across 8 columns totaling $565.00 in rebate amounts. The synthetic data intentionally includes data quality issues: mixed date formats (MM/DD/YYYY and DD-MM-YYYY), inconsistent currency formatting (with and without $ symbols), 6 different status values (including empty), 5 missing deadlines, and 10 missing days-remaining values. Task version 1.0 is deployed on libreoffice_calc_env@0.1 with 180-second timeout and 15-step maximum.
